serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving is approximately 400 g (one bowl), providing around 380 kcal, 30 g protein, 20 g fat, and 20 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB entry exists for 'Braised Bean Curd with Shrimps in Golden Soup'. Values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using HPB Singapore entries for firm tofu (braised), cooked shrimps/prawns, and pumpkin/carrot puree, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for tofu (USDA #16126) and cooked shrimp (USDA #15152). The golden soup base (pumpkin/carrot puree with broth) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central pumpkin puree (#11424) and chicken broth (#6172). Input values from the source record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are retained where consistent with the component-weighted estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — tofu and mixed vegetable soup analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for braised bean curd with shrimps in golden soup. GI estimated at 40 (low) based on analogous foods from the University of Sydney GI Database: firm tofu (GI ~15, very low), pumpkin/carrot puree (GI ~65 for pumpkin alone but diluted in soup matrix), and mixed vegetable-protein composite dishes (GI ~35–45). The dominant low-GI components (tofu, shrimp, broth) suppress the overall GI. Glycemic load per 400 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (40 × 20) / 100 = 8.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g; restaurant preparation may exceed this
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and low-sodium broth when ordering, as the estimated 300 mg sodium per 100 g (1200 mg per 400 g bowl) approaches the 1500 mg daily target; the dish's potassium content (~220 mg per 100 g from pumpkin, tofu, and spring onions) partially offsets sodium's pressor effect, but portion control and seasoning modification are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~40), low carbohydrate (5 g per 100 g), high protein (7.5 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low glycemic index (~40), minimal carbohydrate content (5 g per 100 g), and high protein from tofu and shrimps that slows gastric emptying and attenuates postprandial glucose rise; request minimal cornstarch thickening and pair with a low-GI carbohydrate side such as brown rice to manage overall meal glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 45 mg per 100 g from shrimps; saturated fat low at 0.88 g per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is relatively heart-friendly given its low saturated fat (0.88 g per 100 g) and absence of trans fat, but the shrimp-derived cholesterol (45 mg per 100 g; ~180 mg per 400 g bowl) warrants moderation for patients with established cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia; tofu isoflavones and pumpkin beta-carotene provide cardioprotective antioxidant benefit, and sodium reduction is recommended to support blood pressure management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory ingredients: ginger (gingerols), tofu isoflavones, pumpkin beta-carotene; low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis due to its very low saturated fat (0.88 g per 100 g), anti-inflammatory ginger content, and tofu isoflavones that modulate inflammatory pathways; calcium (62.5 mg per 100 g) supports bone health, and sodium should be kept below 400 mg per 100 g by requesting reduced soy sauce to avoid worsening RA-related tissue oedema.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised Bean Curd with Shrimps in Golden Soup (金汤虾仁烩豆腐, Jīn tāng xiārén huì dòufu) is a comforting Chinese braised dish popular in Singapore's Chinese restaurant and zi char (煮炒) cuisine. The dish features silken or firm tofu and succulent shrimps (prawns) gently braised in a vibrant golden soup base made from blended pumpkin or carrot puree combined with chicken broth, seasoned with soy sauce, ginger, and finished with sesame oil and spring onions. A light cornstarch slurry gives the sauce a silky, coating consistency. The flavour profile is mild, savoury, and slightly sweet from the pumpkin or carrot, with a tender tofu texture and plump, lightly springy shrimp. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 95 kcal, 7.5 g protein, 5.0 g fat (saturated fat 0.88 g), 5.0 g carbohydrate, 1.0 g sugar, 0.75 g dietary fibre, 300 mg sodium, 62.5 mg calcium, and 45 mg cholesterol. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium at 300 mg per 100 g is moderate and warrants attention for hypertension patients. The dish is dairy-free and can be prepared halal. It is commonly served as a shared main dish in a Chinese family-style meal alongside steamed rice.

meal_context: Suitable as part of a balanced Chinese meal with steamed rice

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central entry exists for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of approved-source entries for firm tofu, cooked shrimp, pumpkin puree, and chicken broth. The input record was ai_generated with confidence 0.7; the component-weighted estimate is broadly consistent with the input values, supporting medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using tofu and mixed vegetable-protein composite analogues.
notes: 1. Nutrient values estimated by component-weighted averaging using HPB Singapore and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy approved sources; no exact match found for this composite dish. 2. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific braised composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (1.2 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles for firm tofu (#16126) and cooked shrimp (#15152); these are approximate. 4. potassium_mg (220 mg per 100 g) and vitamin_a_mcg_rae (55 mcg RAE per 100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central pumpkin puree (#11424), tofu, and shrimp component profiles. 5. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Sodium content in restaurant preparations may exceed the estimated 300 mg per 100 g due to variable soy sauce and broth quantities. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for this plant-oil and seafood-based preparation consistent with USDA FoodData Central component data.
